rationale: The synthetic ensures is an implication `P ==> det_eq(r1,r2)`. The prior attempt asserted the per-run ensures (`r1.0 == thread_ptr`) unconditionally, which Verus rejects because r1/r2 are unconstrained parameters. Wrapping the hints in `if P { ... }` makes each assert provable under exactly the antecedent, and the explicit `=~=` plus the final consequent assertion line up the triggers on `r1.1@` / `r2.1@` so z3 can discharge the implication.
